seoTitle: How to Integrate Notion With Jira
metaDescription: Connect Notion with Jira so your documentation and issue tracking stay linked instead of living in separate silos.
intro: Teams often keep planning docs and specs in Notion while tracking execution in Jira, and without a connection between the two, context gets lost. This guide covers linking Notion and Jira together.

steps.heading: Install the Jira integration in Notion
steps.body: Search for the official Jira integration inside Notion's connections settings and start the setup.

steps.heading: Authorize the connection
steps.body: Sign in to your Jira account and grant Notion permission to read issue data.

steps.heading: Embed Jira issues in Notion pages
steps.body: Use the integration to embed live Jira issue links or cards directly inside relevant Notion documents.

steps.heading: Reference Notion pages from Jira
steps.body: Add links to related Notion docs in the Jira issue's description or a dedicated field so context flows both ways.

steps.heading: Keep both up to date
steps.body: Establish a habit of updating both tools as work progresses so the connection stays useful over time.

faq.question: Does Notion show live Jira issue status?
faq.answer: Yes, embedded Jira links in Notion typically display live status information pulled directly from Jira.

faq.question: Is the Notion-Jira integration free to use?
faq.answer: The core integration is generally available without extra cost, though it depends on your Notion and Jira plan tiers.

faq.question: Can I create a Jira issue directly from Notion?
faq.answer: Some integration setups support creating issues from Notion, though the base integration is often focused on linking and embedding.

faq.question: Do I need admin access to set up the integration?
faq.answer: Workspace admin access is often required in Notion, and appropriate permissions are needed on the Jira side as well.
